description: I started using Netsparker when I was a trainee at a cybersecurity firm, and still used it till when I was an associate consultant (I could attend to clients without supervision). This highlights its versatility and ease of use.
author: Aanu B.
pros: While being easy to operate, it is flexible; offering diverse functions in vulnerability assessment from detecting basic vulnerabilities like out-of-date app versions to generating executive style reports of scan results.
cons: It gets quite slow when testing for some vulnerabilities in larger URLs.

description: Great so far, we'll be renewing without a second thought this year. Our organization is looking forward to the recently announced AI scanning capabilities.
author: ryan G.
pros: Invicti Enterprise is a great solution for larger enterprises. The product is easy to set up, with great onboarding documentation. Scans do a great job of filtering out false positives, letting our team focus on actionable items. Lastly, it cannot be understated how well the reports are out of the box compared to a lot of the other products out there.
cons: The cost of the product is prohibitive for smaller organizations. Scan times can get up there as well, which is a drawback of the scan coverage.

description: Overall, my experience with Invicti has been good. However, it could be better if we were able to use the tool’s full capabilities. Some product limitations in certain scenarios make it harder to fully meet our needs.
author: sai L.
pros: Invicti is easy to use, and the platform is very user-friendly. Customer support is very responsive on the tickets.
cons: There are still areas for improvement, and client feedback should be prioritized based on requirements. For example, although we have subscribed to the Auto Discovery of API feature, we have not yet received any feedback or updates on when it will be implemented.

review_title: Great web app scanning tool with great personalized support
description: Mostly a positive experience, especially working our our account manager, who has a solid foundation knowledge of using the platform that does a good job of passing his valuable insights to our weekly meetings.
author: John S.
pros: I like the ability to easily customize our scan policies along with the ability to scan a wide range of API specifications.
cons: Reporting is the area that I would like to see invicti really improve upon. After that, the UI could use a modernization refresh.

review_title: Clear framework with a powerful Web Security Scanner
description: Overall, Accunetix is best suited for web security teams focused on vulnerability detection and compliance.
author: Sarin Nath M.
pros: The interface is intuitive, with well-organized dashboards that make it easy to set up scans and interpret results. Accunetix provides clear, actionable reports and integrates with tools like Jira and Slack for real-time alerts. Notifications are customizable, which helps teams prioritize critical vulnerabilities quickly.
cons: Broad or complex scans can be slow. Involve more scan times for large applications. Sometimes configuring the scan limits, crawling policies etc. are complicated

description: Our experience with Invicti has been overall a positive thing. I have been very satisfied with the capabilities of the tool and how well it integrates with our current setup, including our setup with Mend. The fact that Invicti integrates with Mend is another huge win for us because we use Mend for our SAST and SCA scans.
author: Taylor S.
pros: The tool is very easy to use, and the customer support team is great. I have had quick solutions to my problems each time I have had to reach out to them. The value you get for the product is pretty incredible. We were paying more for another product, and Invicti is a better product, and we are spending less on it.
cons: The onboarding process was a little less hand-holding than I was told it would be, but it wasn't terrible.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The user interface is User friendly and the generated reports is so good and provides good details….
author: Ahmed M.
pros: Fast in scanning, UI is user friendly, also on-primes not on cloud, reports are so good and provide good executive summary
cons: Not scanning all web APP due to security restrictions on the web APP, limitations in API scanning, also some issues in the recording sessions

review_title: Best software for Web Application Vulnerability Management
description: I use Netsparker as a Consultant for my clients. I'm very satisfied about the product and how this software help our client to check the security level of the web applications. We also help our clients to build a remediation plan (through the report of Netsparker is very easy and quick perform that task) and through Netsparker checks that all the remediations implemented are in place
author: Gianluca P.
pros: I like Netsparker because is very easy to use, but at the same time has a lot of options that allow an expert user to customize the level of scanning. Also make authenticated scan is very easy (several options are available). Report templates are different and cover all your needs.
cons: When do concurrent scans it consumes a lot of resources.

review_title: Reliable and Efficient Vulnerability Management Tool
description: Overall, my experience with Invicti was very positive: it made vulnerability management much more straightforward and efficient. Once everything was set up, it provided consistent, actionable results that helped improve security with less manual overhead thus offering a great value for the money.
author: Aurel-Dragoș H.
pros: What I liked most about Invicti was how easy it made finding and prioritizing vulnerabilities without a ton of manual effort. It felt reliable and efficient, which gave me confidence in the results. The customer support was always swift and answered our questions with professionalism and on point.
cons: The part I liked least about Invicti was that the interface sometimes felt a bit cluttered. It also took some time to fine-tune the scans to avoid false positives. But this is to be expected for such a complete product that offers all the relevant functionality.

review_title: Helpful Tool for Managing Application Risks
description: My experience with Invicti is good. It helps me find issues in applications faster and puts everything in one dashboard. I like that it saves time and gives a clear view of risks. Sometimes the reports are a bit too detailed, but overall the tool is useful and makes the job easier
author: Salwa S.
pros: Easy to use and not much false positive. the interface is friendly. The platform pulls together findings from different sources into one dashboard, so security leaders can easily see their overall application risks and manage them more effectively.
cons: Some scans in Invicti are slower, especially for larger applications, and reports can feel too detailed at first. It takes time to adjust and filter the important findings. but it can be accepted.

review_title: Too much money for a third rate product
description: We are required to run weekly vulnerability scans on many of our production websites and we try to use Netsparker to do these scans. We can schedule when the scans will run and they usually do run. The problem is, if the scan has a problem it must be canceled, otherwise it will never finish and no report will ever be generated.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Netsparker comes with an automation API so it is possible to include it as part of a Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) system. It usually works but not always.
cons: There is almost no documentation about how to use the product. You are expected to open a support ticket and ask how to do some of simplest things and it usually take a day to get a response back from support. Often the response is, "We don't support that", or "We don't have a sample that does that". I one time asked for .Net Framework sample code to pull a report from Netsparker and the answer I got was "Go toe the GUI and select these buttons. The report will download." The problem was I needed to download the reports problematically. Other times I asked for .Net samples and was given Python code. They are the same, correct?

review_title: Security testing your software is a must
description: Using netsparker we're able to analyze and test our software on a regular basis. This is essential when our software is available directly on the internet.
author: Jesper T.
pros: Netsparker is pretty intuitive. It simulates a lot of different attacks and vulnerabilities and present the result in a variety of different reports.
cons: It's somewhat expensive, at least if you want the enterprise version. It's not yet possible to ignore specific findings in later scans by marking them as false positives.

description: Stands out in the space as being one of the easier to use tools. While OWASP ZAP is an excellent tool NetSparker takes the same principles to the next level and bundles it into an easy to use and highly valuable application security scanner SaaS!
author: Patrick H.
pros: UI is top-notch and easy to grok. It's highly efficient and customizable tool provide in depth reporting when you need it most. Support team is A+! Being able to generate multiple types of reports based on customer needs is quite useful.
cons: Docs were a bit outdated or not easy to follow when we initially started using the product. Support has steadily been improving them and they look great now.

description: My years of experience with Netsparker have been nothing but positive and I truly enjoy using this tool to assess our web applications.
author: Chris E.
pros: The software is very easy to use yet has extreme amount of customization for scanning any web application.
cons: Without an intimate knowledge of the capability of the tool, you may miss out on truly reaping its benefits in deep scanning of web applications.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is able to scan dynamic single page apps without hours of brittle scripting. The issues it finds are described fully with a clear indication of certainty. Reporting is flexible.
cons: They are not a well known vendor so we have to explain why we use Netsparker vs some of the better known vendors

review_title: NetSparker - Security Vulnerabilty Scanning Tool and Helper
description: Netsparker is comparatively cheaper, and you can bargain i believe, compare to other tools like Accunetix etc. We compare a couple of them and decided this.
author: Valliappan A.
pros: This tool has both web and desktop versions, with some licensing limitations. It scans your website, for issues and reports the issues and ways to fix them, online can leave it to run from cloud and do your work. Their pricing is not that high.
cons: The desktop version when run on your system, it slows down everything in your system, so better to use the cloud base one. But these two versions have some difference i think. But overall we were happier with the package, but later as we did not need much, we did not renew it further.

review_title: Must be there in your list of tools
description: Scanning critical web applications with ease and freedom of configuration.
author: Shashank V.
pros: We use Netsparker for scanning our customers applications. Ease of use and Flexibility gives us freedom to use it wisely and help is completing our projects in timely manner.
cons: Credential scanning and captcha configuration difficulties.

description: We are solving several manual testing with the amazingly accurate automated scanner. Especially with the sign in features for those custom applications that reset session cookies. It logs in with a few custom scripts (which is also easy to implement with Netsparkers element clicking).
author: Kenneth H.
pros: Its accurate findings and overall testing procedures. It enumerates more than other tools do with additional options to boot.
cons: Lack of CSV reporting. I wish I could get the same exact reporting features the Detailed HTML reporting templates do but into a CSV format for easy integration with our own custom reporting templates. I also wish it did more reporting on Serialization attacks. Many web apps are extremely vulnerable to serialization attacks yet Netsparker does not escalate the vulnerability enough (unencrypted viewstate, unsigned viewstate, etc).

description: My overall experience has been positive. I would recommend the software to others and we will continue to use it. Just be ready to play some games with licenses if using the cloud version.
author: Casey R.
pros: Netsparker is easy to use, easy to configure, and you can be up and running your first scan in a matter of minutes. The scan results offer details on how to remediate the findings as well as what they did to find and validate the finding.
cons: One main issues. I would like to be able to change a domain. If I have a license for two domains, I should be able to rotate them. If server1.com goes EoL and server2.com has taken its place, I have to buy another subscription. Not cool.

author: Neil S.
pros: Netsparker helps harden my application by closing out vulnerabilities. We follow an agile development cycle, which includes performing frequent deployments. In the past, we have accidentally deployed vulnerable code, which was quickly fixed once Netsparker has flagged it.
cons: Would be nice if the security scanner were to include scans to help harden the server and OS, but this seems to be strictly a web application scanner. I have to use a second product to scan the OS.

review_title: an easy effective web vulnerability scanner
author: Bilgin M.
pros: it is a easy of use product. It does not use excessive computer resource so you can install it on a laptop. It can give you a detailed vulnerability report.
cons: You have to define target system features but it should discover the target system and it should adapt itself.

review_title: Excellent and Easy in terms of Automated Security Scanning
description: Bugs within web applications and its been an wonderful journey with Netsparker.
author: Antony Arockia Raja S.
pros: Excellent and Easy in terms of Automated Security Scanning and less false positives.
cons: Nothing I can say as Netsparker has been very helpful to our organization in identifying and fixing security vulnerabilities.

review_title: Great value for the price with stellar support
description: We currently use Netsparker as an Automated Web Application Scanner on the products we support. Scans are done on-demand at the end of Sprints as part of our involvement in the Developers SDLC. It has helped us build a good relationship to strengthen security in the products.
author: Daniel C.
pros: Any issues that we contact Netsparker Support on are answered very quickly with detailed information that helps us decide what to do.
cons: There are issues that we end up labeling as False Positive, so that is disappointing. However, it doesn't change my opinion of the product.

author: Sean C.
pros: Easy to setup and get started. Offers on-prem capability linked to the cloud for scanning behind a firewall.
cons: They moved away from being able to offer "packs" of scans where we could easily scan short lived sites. They only offer a subscription model intended for recurring scans.

author: Dennis M.
pros: The reports are well laid out and very comprehensive. One of the best things of the reports are the remediations it provides. The remediations give a lot of detail besides guiding you to a repair.
cons: Sometimes the scans have to be customized due to the size of the application and I had to submit the scan to a Netsparker Engineer for customization.
